Hatsune Miku, officially code-named CV01, is a mascot for a Vocaloid software voicebank developed by Crypton Future Media. She is depicted as a sixteen-year-old girl with long, turquoise twintails. Miku's personification has been marketed as a virtual idol, and has performed at live virtual concerts onstage as an animated holographic projection.
Miku uses Yamaha Corporation's Vocaloid 2, Vocaloid 3, Vocaloid 4, and Vocaloid 6 singing synthesizing technologies, alongside Crypton Future Media's Piapro Studio, a standalone singing synthesizer editor. She was the second Vocaloid sold using the Vocaloid 2 engine and the first Japanese Vocaloid to use the Japanese version of the 2 engine. Her voice is based on samples taken from Japanese voice actress Saki Fujita.
The name of the character comes from merging the Japanese words for first, sound, and future, thus meaning "the first sound of the future", which, along with her code name, refers to her position as the first of Crypton's "Character Vocal Series", preceding Kagamine Rin/Len and Megurine Luka. The number 01 can be seen on her left shoulder in official artwork.

From Wikipedia (

microformatDataRenderer.title: Hatsune Miku
microformatDataRenderer.description:
Hatsune Miku, officially code-named CV01, is a mascot for a Vocaloid software voicebank developed by Crypton Future Media. She is depicted as a sixteen-year-old girl with long, turquoise twintails. Miku's personification has been marketed as a virtual idol, and has performed at live virtual concerts onstage as an animated holographic projection.
Miku uses Yamaha Corporation's Vocaloid 2, Vocaloid 3, Vocaloid 4, and Vocaloid 6 singing synthesizing technologies, alongside Crypton Future Media's Piapro Studio, a standalone singing synthesizer editor. She was the second Vocaloid sold using the Vocaloid 2 engine and the first Japanese Vocaloid to use the Japanese version of the 2 engine. Her voice is based on samples taken from Japanese voice actress Saki Fujita.
The name of the character comes from merging the Japanese words for first, sound, and future, thus meaning "the first sound of the future", which, along with her code name, refers to her position as the first of Crypton's "Character Vocal Series", preceding Kagamine Rin/Len and Megurine Luka. The number 01 can be seen on her left shoulder in official artwork.
